# adr-system

<!-- awf:edit current-state: from .awf/domains/parts/adr-system/current-state.md -->
## Current state

Decisions live as append-only ADRs under `docs/decisions/`, parsed by `internal/adr` (a shared `internal/frontmatter` parser) into a generated `ACTIVE.md` status index, regenerated by `awf sync` and drift-checked by a regenerate-and-compare path. Append-only protects rationale, not orthography (ADR-0115): an Implemented ADR's arguments, decision items, alternatives, and consequences are never rewritten, but a meaning-preserving punctuation fix to its `# ` heading line is normalization rather than revision, and is permitted with explicit maintainer approval. ADR-0118 widened that carve-out, partially superseding ADR-0115 Decision item 5: retroactive normalization reaches the full authored body of every ADR and plan, not the heading alone, and the whole corpus was swept to plain punctuation in one act (roughly 7200 sites across 182 files, fences included). Two exemptions stand, both named rather than inferred: ADR-0113, the ADR about the em-dash, by maintainer designation; and prose where the glyph is the subject of its own sentence, which punctuating would make false. What licenses a sweep that large over settled rationale is that word preservation is proven rather than promised: a word-stream comparison (alphanumeric runs only, old versus new) must report zero delta, so the risk is a flat punctuation choice, never lost argument. Each ADR declares `domains:`, which drives this doc's generated `## Decisions` index (same regenerate-and-compare mechanism). The workflow chain (brainstorm → ADR → plan → resync → impl → review) is enforced by the `awf-*` skills and reviewer agents. The ADR guide (`README.md`), the skeleton (`template.md`), and a plan-authoring guide (`plans/README.md`) are awf-managed singletons (ADR-0021, ADR-0020), so the `.layout.adrReadme` / `.layout.adrTemplate` / `.layout.plansReadme` references resolve in any adopter project. `ACTIVE.md` always renders (a placeholder index when no ADRs exist) so its document-map link resolves out of the box (ADR-0020, partial-item supersedence of ADR-0005/ADR-0006). A new ADR is scaffolded via `awf new adr "<title>"` (ADR-0042), which computes the next sequential number and copies `template.md` with its marker comments stripped and its date/title filled in, rather than by hand; `awf-proposing-adr` invokes it as the first authoring step. ADR-0103 revives the long-dropped `tags:` and `related:` frontmatter: `internal/adr` now lifts both into `adr.ADR`, `awf check` resolves each ADR's `related:` numbers against the decisions dir (`invariant: adr-related-link-resolved`, the ADR analogue of the pitfall/plan link checks), and the corpus is tagged from a governed vocabulary, the relevance currency a later slice will spend in `awf context`. Supersession is structured, machine-checked, and has exactly one encoding (ADR-0120, generalised by ADR-0128, which removed the `supersedes:`/`superseded_by:` frontmatter keys entirely). A claim is an inline Decision-section token, one per claimed **anchor** - a Decision item or a declared invariant slug - with both the anchor kind and the relation named by the key: `supersedes: ADR-NNNN#<item>` and `supersedes-invariant: ADR-NNNN#<slug>` **retire** an anchor, `refines: ADR-NNNN#<item>` **adapts** one. The split is corpus-driven: adapting a decision turned out to be roughly twice as common as replacing it, and conflating the two would retire ADRs nobody had superseded. Slug anchors have no refinement form, a slug being atomic. Full supersession is then *derived*, not declared: an ADR is `Superseded` exactly when every one of its anchors carries a retirement from an `Implemented` carrier, so each decision must be retired explicitly with its rationale sitting in the Decision item that carries the token. The status is hand-written and bare - it names no successor, because coverage may split across successors and a scalar would lie - and `awf check` refuses drift in both directions, naming the exact edit. `awf check` validates every ref (the target exists, is not `Proposed`, and owns the cited anchor) and enforces the **back-pointer** ADR-0116 Decision 5 had declined pending evidence, now owed for targets of **any** status (ADR-0128 item 5): without that widening a claimant landing after the flip owed nothing and became invisible, and the bare status has nothing else to recover it from. Item anchors stay stable because every ADR's Decision section must be column-0 numbered items sequential from 1, checked regardless of status. What the checker deliberately does not judge is rationale *quality*: it guarantees a rationale site exists per claim and no more, leaving the judgment to the `adr-reviewer` lens. One anchor claimed by two live ADRs degrades to an advisory note; a token claiming its own carrier's anchor, and a retirement cycle among fully covered ADRs, are errors (ADR-0129 item 7). ADR-0129 derives all of this from one anchor-coverage model built inside the corpus view (ADR-0130): anchors are nodes, claims are edges carrying the relation and the Decision item that justifies them, and `Live`/`PartiallySuperseded`/`Covered` fall out of it. `ACTIVE.md` renders chains one-to-many and the claimed anchors on live ADRs, the per-domain indexes name the claiming ADR numbers, and `awf context` annotates surfaced entries with theirs. ADR-0116's append-only reconciliation stands - `status` **and** cross-reference metadata (`superseded_by:`, `related:`) are editable in place, append-only protecting rationale, not bookkeeping - refined by the ADR-0120 item 9 carve-out: a live body's *meaning* is frozen, and a meaning-preserving schema retrofit may migrate its machine-readable encoding, the license under which `awf upgrade`'s generation-10 migration rewrote `retires_invariants:` frontmatter into bookkeeping Decision items corpus-wide, and its generation-12 successor stripped the two supersession keys, downgraded every pre-existing item token to `refines:`, and appended a bookkeeping item retiring each legacy predecessor's anchors. ADR-0120 item 11 commits the corpus retrofit that retires ADR-0116 Decision 6's known non-conformance: freeform supersedes-prose citations gain tokens and the deferred back-pointer edges are backfilled. ADR Decision syntax ignores fenced examples for supersession checks and migrations.
